name: 00 — Header & Table of Contents
content:
================================================================================
        EPSILON AIRLINES CUSTOMER SERVICE MANUAL
        Publication EA-CSM-2025: Policies, Procedures,
        and Fare Regulations for All Service Classes
        Effective January 1, 2025
================================================================================

TABLE OF CONTENTS
Section A: Fare Classes & Ticket Types
Section B: Baggage Policies & Fees
Section C: Voyager Rewards Loyalty Program
Section D: Flight Changes, Cancellations & Refunds
Section E: Seating, Upgrades & UpLift Tokens
Section F: Special Assistance & Accessibility Services
Section G: Stratosphere Lounge Access & Amenities
Section H: Irregular Operations & Passenger Protections
Section I: International Travel Regulations & Requirements
Section J: Aircraft Fleet & Route Network
Section K: Digital Services & Technology
Section L: Travel Insurance & Protection Plans
Section M: Safety, Security & Compliance
Section N: Corporate & Group Travel Programs
Appendix A: SkyCredits Earning & Redemption Tables
Appendix B: Fare Class Comparison Matrix
Appendix C: EpsilonConnect Corporate Rate Schedule

IMPORTANT NOTICE: This publication constitutes the complete and authoritative
reference for Epsilon Airlines customer-facing policies, fare regulations,
loyalty program rules, and service standards. All monetary amounts are expressed
in United States Dollars (USD). The policies herein are effective as of January
1, 2025, and supersede all prior versions of the Epsilon Airlines Customer
Service Manual including EA-CSM-2024 and all interim policy bulletins issued
during calendar year 2024.

For questions regarding this publication, contact Epsilon Airlines Customer
Support at 1-800-EPSILON (1-800-377-4566) or visit www.epsilonairlines.com/support.

name: 01 — Fare Classes & Ticket Types
content:
SECTION A: FARE CLASSES & TICKET TYPES

Epsilon Airlines offers four primary fare classes, each with distinct service levels, baggage allowances, change and cancellation policies, and SkyCredits earning rates.

A.1 ESSENTIALS FARE
The Essentials Fare is Epsilon Airlines' lowest published fare and is subject to the most restrictive terms of carriage.

A.1.1 Seat Assignment
Essentials Fare passengers do NOT select a seat at the time of booking. Seats are assigned automatically at check-in (beginning 24 hours before scheduled departure). The system assigns middle seats first (seats B and E on Epsilon's standard 3-3 configuration).
Exception: Voyager Rewards members at the Navigator tier or above may pre-select a Standard seat at booking even when holding an Essentials Fare ticket.

A.1.2 Carry-On Baggage
Essentials Fare passengers are permitted ONE personal item that must fit entirely beneath the seat in front of them. Maximum personal item dimensions: 18 x 14 x 8 inches (45 x 36 x 20 cm).
A full-size carry-on bag (22 x 14 x 9 inches) is NOT included with the Essentials Fare. Passengers who bring an overhead-bin-sized bag to the gate will be required to check the bag and pay the applicable checked baggage fee of $35 plus a $25 gate-handling surcharge (total $60).

A.1.3 Checked Baggage
Essentials Fare does not include any free checked baggage. Checked bag fees for Essentials Fare passengers are:
1st checked bag: $35, 2nd checked bag: $45, 3rd checked bag: $150, Each additional: $200
Maximum weight per bag: 50 lbs (23 kg). Overweight bags (51-70 lbs) incur an additional $100 surcharge per bag. Bags exceeding 70 lbs (32 kg) are not accepted.

A.1.4 Changes and Same-Day Modifications
Advance changes (more than 24 hours before departure): Change fee: $39 per passenger per direction. Fare difference: Required. If the new fare is lower, no credit is issued.
Same-day changes: Essentials Fare passengers are NOT eligible for same-day confirmed changes. Same-day standby is permitted at the airport only, subject to a $75 same-day standby fee.

A.1.5 Cancellations and Refunds
Essentials Fare tickets are NON-REFUNDABLE. Upon cancellation, the passenger receives a future travel credit equal to the original fare minus a $99 cancellation processing fee. Future travel credits expire 12 months from the date of issue and are non-transferable.
24-Hour Flexible Booking Rule: Any ticket — including Essentials Fare — purchased at least 7 days before departure may be cancelled within 24 hours of booking for a full refund to the original form of payment with no fees.

A.1.6 SkyCredits Earning Rate
Essentials Fare passengers earn SkyCredits at 50% of the base fare (excluding taxes and fees).
Base earning: 50% of base fare in SkyCredits. Explorer tier bonus: None (1.0x multiplier). Navigator tier bonus: 1.25x multiplier (effective rate: 62.5%). Captain tier bonus: 1.50x multiplier (effective rate: 75%). Admiral tier bonus: 2.00x multiplier (effective rate: 100%).

A.1.7 Upgrades
Essentials Fare tickets are NOT eligible for complimentary upgrades, UpLift Token upgrades, or SkyCredits upgrade requests.
Exception: Admiral tier members may use UpLift Tokens to upgrade from Essentials Fare to Horizon Fare only (not directly to Summit or Apex), per Section E.4.3.

A.2 HORIZON FARE
The Horizon Fare is Epsilon Airlines' standard economy class product, offering a balance of value and flexibility. It is the most commonly booked fare class across the Epsilon Airlines network.

A.2.1 Seat Assignment
Horizon Fare passengers may select a Standard seat at the time of booking at no additional charge. SkyStretch Seats are available for an additional fee: Domestic $45, Medium-haul international $65, Long-haul international $89.

A.2.2 Carry-On and Checked Baggage
Carry-on: ONE full-size carry-on bag (22 x 14 x 9 inches) plus ONE personal item included at no charge.
Checked baggage: 1st checked bag: FREE (up to 50 lbs / 23 kg), 2nd checked bag: $45, 3rd checked bag: $150.

A.2.3 Changes and Same-Day Modifications
Navigator tier and above: $0 change fee. Explorer tier / Non-members: $49 change fee. Fare difference required in all cases.
Same-day confirmed changes: $0 for Captain tier and above; $75 for all others. Same-day standby: Permitted at no charge for all Horizon Fare passengers.

A.2.4 Cancellations and Refunds
Horizon Fare tickets are refundable subject to a $75 cancellation fee. Alternatively, the passenger may elect a future travel credit for the full fare amount (no cancellation fee deducted).

A.2.5 SkyCredits Earning Rate
Base earning: 100% of base fare in SkyCredits. Explorer tier: 1.0x, Navigator tier: 1.25x, Captain tier: 1.50x, Admiral tier: 2.00x.

A.2.6 Upgrades
Horizon Fare passengers are eligible for: (a) Complimentary upgrades to Summit Fare for Captain tier and above, beginning 24 hours before departure. (b) UpLift Token upgrades from Horizon to Summit (1 token per segment) or Horizon to Apex Class (2 tokens domestic; 3 tokens international over 2,500 miles). (c) SkyCredits upgrade bids via the "UpBid" program.

A.3 SUMMIT FARE
The Summit Fare is Epsilon Airlines' premium economy product, providing enhanced comfort and amenities.

A.3.1 Seat Assignment — SkyStretch Seats
All Summit Fare passengers are seated in the SkyStretch section: Seat pitch of 36-38 inches (vs 30-31 in Standard), Seat width of 18.5 inches (vs 17.2), Adjustable headrest with side wings, Dedicated overhead bin space, Power outlet (AC and USB-C) at every seat, 13.3-inch personal seatback entertainment screen.

A.3.3 Carry-On and Checked Baggage
Checked baggage: 1st checked bag: FREE, 2nd checked bag: FREE, 3rd checked bag: $150.

A.3.5 Cancellations and Refunds
Summit Fare tickets are FULLY REFUNDABLE. No cancellation fee applies. Passengers may also elect a future travel credit with a 10% bonus.

A.3.6 SkyCredits Earning Rate
Base earning: 150% of base fare in SkyCredits. Admiral tier bonus: 2.00x multiplier (effective rate: 300%).

A.3.7 Complimentary Amenities
Summit Fare passengers receive: Complimentary premium snack box on flights over 2 hours, Complimentary non-alcoholic beverages including specialty coffee, Complimentary alcoholic beverages on flights over 3 hours, Noise-canceling headphones on flights over 4 hours.

A.4 APEX CLASS
Apex Class is Epsilon Airlines' premium cabin, featuring the CloudDeck Suite on widebody international aircraft and recliner-style seating on domestic and narrowbody routes.

A.4.1 CloudDeck Suite (International Widebody)
On Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner and Airbus A330-900neo aircraft: Fully lie-flat seat converting to a 78-inch bed, Suite door for personal privacy, Direct aisle access from every seat (1-2-1 configuration), 18-inch 4K personal entertainment screen, Premium bedding set (Frette duvet, memory foam mattress pad).

A.4.4 Carry-On and Checked Baggage
1st, 2nd, 3rd checked bag: FREE (up to 70 lbs / 32 kg each). 4th and beyond: $200 each.

A.4.6 Cancellations and Refunds
Apex Class tickets are FULLY REFUNDABLE at any time — including after departure for the unused portion.

A.4.7 SkyCredits Earning Rate
Base earning: 200% of base fare. Admiral tier: 2.00x (effective rate: 400%).

A.4.8 Stratosphere Lounge Access
All Apex Class passengers receive complimentary access to any Stratosphere Lounge worldwide including partner airline lounges in the CloudAlliance network. One guest may accompany the Apex Class passenger at no charge.

A.5 GROUP BOOKINGS (10 OR MORE PASSENGERS)
Group rates available on Horizon, Summit, and Apex Class only. Discounts: 10-19 passengers: 5%, 20-49: 8%, 50-99: 10%, 100+: Negotiated rate.

A.6 CORPORATE ACCOUNTS — EPSILONCONNECT PROGRAM
EpsilonConnect Standard: 200 annual segments, 3% discount. EpsilonConnect Plus: 500 annual segments, 5% discount on Horizon/Summit, 3% on Apex. EpsilonConnect Premier: 1,500 annual segments, negotiated custom rates 8-15% below published fares, dedicated reservations phone line, complimentary Stratosphere Lounge day passes.

A.7 FARE CLASS COMBINABILITY AND MIXED-CABIN ITINERARIES

A.7.1 Mixed-Cabin Bookings
Epsilon Airlines permits mixed-cabin itineraries where different segments of a round-trip or multi-city itinerary are booked in different fare classes. For example, a passenger may book the outbound segment in Horizon Fare and the return segment in Summit Fare.

Rules governing mixed-cabin bookings:
- Baggage allowance: The more generous baggage allowance applies to the entire itinerary when booked on a single ticket (single PNR).
- SkyCredits earning: Each segment earns SkyCredits at the rate corresponding to its fare class. No averaging or upgrading of earning rates across segments.
- Change fees: Each segment is subject to the change fee policy of its respective fare class.
- Upgrades: Upgrade eligibility is determined per-segment based on the fare class of that segment.
- Lounge access: Applicable only for segments booked in Apex Class (or by tier status). A Horizon outbound does not grant lounge access even if the return is in Apex.

A.7.2 Round-Trip Pricing vs. One-Way Pricing
Epsilon Airlines uses one-way pricing for all fare classes. A round-trip ticket is priced as the sum of two one-way fares. There is no round-trip discount. This applies to all booking channels including the website, app, and travel agencies.

Exception: Certain promotional fares may be offered as round-trip only. These are clearly marked during the booking process and require both outbound and return segments to be booked simultaneously.

A.7.3 Child and Infant Fares
Children (ages 2-11): Full fare in all fare classes. Children occupy their own seat and are subject to all standard policies.
Infants (under age 2): Lap infant fare is 10% of the adult fare on domestic flights and 10% of the adult fare plus applicable taxes on international flights. Lap infants do not receive a seat, baggage allowance, or meal service. One lap infant per accompanying adult. Infants may be booked in their own seat at the full child fare; a car seat approved for aircraft use is required.

A.7.4 Standby Travel and Revenue Standby
Revenue standby (traveling on an earlier or later flight on the same day) is available as follows:
- Essentials Fare: Not eligible for same-day standby.
- Horizon Fare: Same-day standby permitted. Fee: $30 for Explorer/non-members, free for Navigator tier and above.
- Summit Fare: Same-day standby permitted at no charge for all passengers.
- Apex Class: Same-day standby permitted at no charge. Apex Class standby passengers are given priority over other standby categories.

Standby passengers are cleared in the following priority order:
1. Apex Class standby passengers (in tier order)
2. Summit Fare standby passengers (in tier order)
3. Horizon Fare standby passengers with fee paid or fee waiver (in tier order)
Standby clearing occurs approximately 30 minutes before departure. Uncleared standby passengers retain their original confirmed booking.

A.7.5 Ticket Validity and Routing Rules
All Epsilon Airlines tickets are valid for travel on Epsilon Airlines and Epsilon Express operated flights only, unless the ticket specifically includes a codeshare segment operated by a CloudAlliance partner airline.
- Tickets are valid for 12 months from the date of issue.
- Open-jaw itineraries (flying into one city and departing from another) are permitted on Horizon, Summit, and Apex Class at no additional charge.
- Circle trips and multi-stop itineraries (up to 4 segments) may be booked on the website or through reservations. Pricing is the sum of one-way fares for each segment.
- Stopovers (staying at a connecting point for more than 24 hours) are available on international itineraries in Summit and Apex Class only, at no additional charge. One stopover permitted per direction.

name: 02 — Baggage Policies
content:
SECTION B: BAGGAGE POLICIES & FEES

B.1 CARRY-ON ALLOWANCE

B.1.1 Personal Item
All passengers, regardless of fare class, are permitted one (1) personal item free of charge. The personal item must not exceed 18 x 14 x 8 inches (46 x 36 x 20 cm) and must fit entirely beneath the seat in front of the passenger.

B.1.2 Carry-On Bag
Passengers traveling on Horizon Fare, Summit Fare, or Apex Class tickets are entitled to one (1) carry-on bag free of charge. The carry-on bag must not exceed 22 x 14 x 9 inches (56 x 36 x 23 cm). Passengers holding Essentials Fare tickets must pay a fee of $35.00 per carry-on bag.

B.1.3 Gate-Check Policy
When overhead bin space is exhausted, Epsilon Airlines will gate-check carry-on bags at no additional charge. Passengers who voluntarily gate-check their carry-on bag prior to boarding may receive a $10.00 SkyCredit bonus.

B.1.4 Carry-On Compliance Enforcement
Gate agents are authorized to measure carry-on bags using the sizer located at each boarding gate. Bags that do not fit the sizer will be checked and the applicable fee applied.

B.2 CHECKED BAGGAGE ALLOWANCES BY FARE CLASS

B.2.1 Essentials Fare: Zero (0) complimentary checked bags. First checked bag: $35.00, Second: $50.00, Third and each additional: $75.00 each. All checked bags must not exceed 50 lbs (23 kg) in weight and 62 linear inches (158 cm).

B.2.2 Horizon Fare: One (1) complimentary checked bag. Second: $50.00, Third+: $75.00.

B.2.3 Summit Fare: Two (2) complimentary checked bags. Third: $50.00, Fourth+: $75.00.

B.2.4 Apex Class: Three (3) complimentary checked bags, each permitted up to 70 lbs (32 kg). Additional bags: $75.00 each.

B.2.6 Prepaid Baggage Discount
Passengers who purchase checked baggage at the time of booking or during online check-in receive a $5.00 discount per bag.

B.3 OVERWEIGHT BAGS
51-70 lbs: $75.00 surcharge per bag. 71-100 lbs: $150.00 surcharge per bag. Over 100 lbs: NOT ACCEPTED — must be shipped via Epsilon Cargo Services.

B.4 OVERSIZED BAGS
63-80 linear inches: $100.00 surcharge. 81-115 linear inches: $200.00 surcharge. Over 115 linear inches: NOT ACCEPTED as checked baggage.

B.5 SPECIAL ITEMS
B.5.1 Sporting Equipment: Golf bags: $45.00 per set. Skis/snowboards: $45.00. Bicycles: $75.00 (must be boxed). Surfboards: $100.00 per board.
B.5.2 Musical Instruments: Small instruments that fit carry-on dimensions may be brought aboard free. Large instruments require purchase of an additional seat at 75% of the applicable fare.
B.5.3 Fragile Items: Liability limited to $50.00 per pound. Passengers must sign Limited Liability Release Form.

B.6 PET TRAVEL AS BAGGAGE
In-Cabin: Small dogs and cats, $125.00 one-way, max 2 pets per flight. Carrier must fit under seat (17 x 11 x 7.5 inches).
Cargo: $250.00 one-way, IATA-compliant kennel required.
Service Animals: Travel free, not subject to cabin pet limits.

B.7 LOST, DELAYED, AND DAMAGED BAGGAGE
Domestic flights: report within 4 hours. International: within 21 days.
Domestic liability: $3,800.00 max per passenger. International: ~$1,780.00 per Montreal Convention.
Delayed baggage: $50.00 per day for essential purchases, max 5 days ($250.00 total).
Damaged baggage: repair or replace up to $500.00 fair market value.

B.8 LOYALTY TIER BAGGAGE BENEFITS
Explorer: No additional benefits. Navigator: +1 free checked bag. Captain: +2 free checked bags, first overweight bag surcharge waived, priority bag tags. Admiral: +3 free checked bags, all overweight surcharges waived (up to 70 lbs), priority bag tags, guaranteed first-off-aircraft delivery at hub airports.
SkyCredit earning on baggage fees: 5 SkyCredits per $1.00 spent.

name: 03 — Voyager Rewards Loyalty Program
content:
SECTION C: VOYAGER REWARDS LOYALTY PROGRAM

C.1 PROGRAM OVERVIEW
The Voyager Rewards program is Epsilon Airlines' loyalty program, designed to reward frequent travelers with SkyCredits, tier status benefits, and exclusive privileges. Membership is free and open to all individuals aged 18 or older.

C.1.2 Program Currency
(a) SkyCredits (SC): The primary earning and redemption currency. Earned through flights, partner purchases, and credit card spending, redeemable for flights, upgrades, merchandise, and experiences.
(b) UpLift Tokens: Upgrade-specific tokens awarded to Captain and Admiral tier members. Used exclusively for cabin class upgrades.

C.2 EARNING SKYCREDITS
Base Earning Rates by Fare Class: Essentials Fare: 5 SC per $1.00 spent. Horizon Fare: 8 SC per $1.00. Summit Fare: 12 SC per $1.00. Apex Class: 16 SC per $1.00.
Admiral Tier: Earns SkyCredits on total ticket price including taxes and fees, not just base fare.
Tier Bonus Multipliers: Explorer: 0% bonus. Navigator: 40% bonus. Captain: 80% bonus. Admiral: 120% bonus.
Partner Earning: Hotels: 3 SC/$1. Car rental: 2 SC/$1. Dining: 4 SC/$1.

C.3 EPSILON VOYAGER CREDIT CARD
Standard Card ($95/yr after first year free): 1.5 SC per $1 general, 3 SC per $1 Epsilon, 2 SC per $1 travel. Sign-up bonus: 60,000 SC after $4,000 in 90 days.
Premium Card ($495/yr): 2 SC per $1 general, 5 SC per $1 Epsilon, 3 SC per $1 travel. Annual 15,000 SC bonus. $200 annual travel credit. Sign-up bonus: 90,000 SC after $5,000 in 90 days. Includes Stratosphere Lounge access and 2 free checked bags.

C.4 TIER QUALIFICATION
Explorer: 0 TQF / 0 TQSC (default). Navigator: 25 TQF or 30,000 TQSC. Captain: 50 TQF or 75,000 TQSC. Admiral: 85 TQF or 140,000 TQSC.
Qualification year: March 1 through last day of February.
Members who achieve a tier retain it for the remainder of the qualification year plus the entirety of the following year (12-24 months of benefits).
Lifetime Status: 2,000,000 lifetime SkyCredits = permanent Admiral tier status.

C.5 TIER BENEFITS
SkyCredit Bonus: Explorer 0%, Navigator 40%, Captain 80%, Admiral 120%.
Complimentary Upgrades: Explorer No, Navigator Standby (24 hrs), Captain Confirmed (48 hrs), Admiral Confirmed (72 hrs).
Stratosphere Lounge: Captain Yes (+1 guest), Admiral Yes (+2 guests).
Additional Free Checked Bags: Navigator +1, Captain +2, Admiral +3.
Priority Boarding: Explorer Zone 5, Navigator Zone 3, Captain Zone 2, Admiral Zone 1.
UpLift Tokens (Annual): Captain 4, Admiral 8.
SkyCredit Expiration Waiver: Admiral only.
Complimentary SkyStretch Seats: Captain (48 hrs prior), Admiral (at booking).
Confirmed CloudDeck Upgrades: Admiral only (72 hrs prior).

C.6 UPLIFT TOKENS
Captain tier: 4 UpLift Tokens per qualification year. Admiral tier: 8 UpLift Tokens per qualification year.
Redemption: Horizon to Summit: 1 token. Summit to Apex: 2 tokens. Horizon to Apex: 3 tokens.
Essentials Fare tickets are NOT eligible for UpLift Token upgrades.
Tokens valid 12 months from issue, non-transferable. Max 3 tokens per one-way itinerary.

C.7 SKYCREDIT REDEMPTION
Flight Redemptions (one-way Horizon): Domestic: 12,500 SC. Transatlantic: 30,000 SC. Transpacific: 40,000 SC. Caribbean/Mexico: 15,000 SC. South America: 25,000 SC.
Higher cabin: Summit 1.5x, Apex 2.5x of Horizon rate.
Upgrade Redemptions: Domestic Horizon to Summit: 8,000 SC. Domestic Summit to Apex: 15,000 SC. International Horizon to Summit: 15,000 SC. International Summit to Apex: 25,000 SC.
Merchandise: 1 SC = $0.008 (125 SC = $1.00).
Experiences: Airport tours 15,000 SC, Flight simulator 25,000 SC, Cockpit visits 20,000 SC.
SkyCredits + Cash: Min 5,000 SC, shortfall at $0.012 per SC.

C.8 SKYCREDIT EXPIRATION
SkyCredits expire after 24 consecutive months of account inactivity. Any qualifying activity resets the clock.
Admiral tier members are exempt from expiration while maintaining Admiral status.
Accounts with no activity for 36 months may be closed. Reactivation possible within 60 days.

name: 04 — Changes, Cancellations & Refunds
content:
SECTION D: BOOKING CHANGES, CANCELLATIONS & REFUNDS

D.1 24-HOUR FREE CANCELLATION POLICY
Full refund on any fare class when cancellation is made within 24 hours of booking, provided the departure date is 7 or more days from the date of booking. Credit card refunds within 7 business days; debit card within 10. SkyCredits refunded within 24 hours.
Does not apply to bookings made fewer than 7 days before departure.

D.2 CHANGE FEES BY FARE CLASS
Essentials Fare: $99 per passenger per direction, plus fare difference. No credit if new fare is lower. Changes not permitted within 24 hours of departure.
Horizon Fare: $49 per passenger per direction. Navigator+ tier: $0 change fee. If new fare is lower, difference issued as 12-month travel credit.
Summit Fare: $0 change fee. If new fare is lower, difference refunded to original payment within 10 business days.
Apex Class: $0 change fee. Unlimited changes. If new fare is lower, refunded within 7 business days. Changes may be made up to 1 hour before departure.

D.3 SAME-DAY CHANGES
Available for Horizon, Summit, and Apex. Essentials not eligible.
Same-Day Confirmed Change: Captain/Admiral: $0. Others: $75. Apex: $0 regardless of tier.
Requirements: Same calendar date, same origin/destination, request at least 1 hour before original departure.
Same-Day Standby: Navigator+: free. Explorer/non-members on Horizon+: $30. Cleared in tier order, 30 minutes before departure.

D.4 CANCELLATION & REFUND RULES
Essentials: No cash refund. Travel credit issued for ticket value minus $99 fee. Valid 12 months.
Horizon: Refund to original payment minus $75 cancellation fee. Processed within 10 business days.
Summit: Full refund with no deductions. Within 7 business days.
Apex: Full refund with no deductions. Within 5 business days. UpLift Tokens returned within 24 hours.
Travel Credit Transferability: May be applied toward new booking for any passenger originally named on the reservation. Not transferable to others. Cannot combine credits.

D.5 INVOLUNTARY CHANGES (AIRLINE-INITIATED)
Schedule Change >60 min: Full refund or free rebooking within 7 days.
Schedule Change 30-60 min: Rebooking on next available flight, no refund.
Schedule Change <30 min: No compensation, itinerary auto-updated.
Equipment Swap Downgrade: Fare difference refunded + 500 SkyCredits goodwill.

D.6 WEATHER & FORCE MAJEURE EVENTS
All passengers may rebook free within 14 days, no fare difference. If no flights available within 14 days, full-value travel credit for 12 months.
Epsilon does NOT provide hotel/meal vouchers for Force Majeure. Exception: Captain and Admiral tier receive $50 meal voucher.

D.7 NAME CHANGES
Minor corrections (up to 3 characters): Free, must be 24+ hours before departure.
Full name changes: Not permitted. Must cancel and rebook.
Middle name and suffix changes: Free at any time before departure.

D.8 TICKET VALIDITY & TRAVEL CREDIT EXPIRATION
All tickets valid for travel within 12 months from issue. Travel credits valid 12 months from cancellation/issuance.
Reinstatement of Expired Credits (Admiral Only): Within 30 days of expiration, $50 reinstatement fee, additional 6 months validity. One reinstatement per credit.

name: 05 — Upgrades & Seating
content:
SECTION E: UPGRADES & SEAT SELECTION

E.1 SEAT CATEGORIES
Standard Seats: Pitch 31-32 inches, Width 17.2-17.8 inches. Rows 15-38.
Preferred Seats: Aisle/window in rows 6-14. Surcharge $15-$45 depending on route distance. Complimentary for Captain and Admiral tier.
SkyStretch Seats: Pitch 34-36 inches, Width 18.0-18.5 inches. Rows 1-5. Surcharge: Under 1,500mi $45, 1,500-3,000mi $65, Over 3,000mi $89. Included free for Summit Fare. Captain tier: 50% discount on Horizon. Admiral tier: complimentary on Horizon.
CloudDeck Suite: Lie-flat 78-inch bed, 22-inch width, direct aisle access in 1-2-1 configuration. Available on wide-body 2,500+ mile routes. Exclusively with Apex Class or via upgrade.

E.2 SEAT SELECTION RULES BY FARE CLASS
Essentials: Assigned at check-in. May purchase: Standard $9-$15, Preferred $15-$45, SkyStretch $45-$89.
Horizon: Free Standard seat at booking. May upgrade to Preferred or SkyStretch.
Summit: Free SkyStretch seat at booking.
Apex: CloudDeck Suite assigned at booking. May select specific seat via Manage Booking.

E.3 UPGRADE METHODS
Cash Upgrades: Horizon to Summit: $89-$159/segment. Any fare to Apex: $299-$899/segment. Gate upgrades $25-$75 higher.
SkyCredits Upgrades: Horizon to Summit: 8,000-15,000 SC. Any to Apex: 25,000-50,000 SC. Submit up to 120 hours before departure.
UpLift Token Upgrades: 1 token: Horizon to Summit. 2 tokens: Summit to Apex or Horizon to Apex (1 segment). Captain 4 tokens/year, Admiral 8 tokens/year. Submit 120 to 24 hours before departure.
Complimentary Upgrades: Captain: confirmed 24 hours before departure. Admiral: confirmed 72 hours before departure. Next cabin class, subject to availability.

E.4 UPGRADE PRIORITY & WAITLIST
Priority 1: Admiral requesting Apex. Priority 2: Captain requesting Apex. Priority 3: Admiral requesting Summit. Priority 4: Captain requesting Summit. Priority 5: UpLift Token requests. Priority 6: SkyCredits requests. Priority 7: Cash requests.
Tiebreaking: Higher tier > Earlier enrollment > Earlier check-in.
Waitlist processed continuously until 30 minutes before departure. Uncleared requests cancelled, tokens/credits returned within 24 hours.

E.5 COMPLIMENTARY UPGRADE RULES
Only for Horizon and Summit fares (not Essentials). Only domestic and short-haul international under 2,500 miles. Not available on codeshare flights. Must have Voyager Rewards number on booking.

E.6 FAMILY SEATING POLICY
Children under 14: Epsilon makes reasonable efforts to seat adjacent to accompanying adult at no extra charge. Guaranteed on Horizon+. Essentials: best effort at check-in. May upgrade child to Preferred Seat at no charge to achieve adjacency.

name: 06 — Delays & Irregular Operations
content:
SECTION F: FLIGHT DELAYS, CANCELLATIONS & IRREGULAR OPERATIONS

F.1 DELAY COMPENSATION (airline-controlled causes: mechanical, crew scheduling, operational decisions)
Weather, ATC, and security events are excluded from compensation.

F.1.2 Delay Tiers and Benefits
(a) 1 hour to 1:59: $12 meal voucher.
(b) 2 hours to 3:59: $25 meal voucher. Captain/Admiral get Stratosphere Lounge access.
(c) 4 hours to 5:59: $50 meal voucher. 1,000 SkyCredits deposited within 72 hours. Complimentary Wi-Fi on delayed/rebooked flight.
(d) 6 hours or more: $100 Epsilon travel credit (valid 12 months). 2,500 SkyCredits. Hotel accommodation if overnight stay required. Ground transportation to/from hotel.

Non-Voyager members: SkyCredits held in escrow for 90 days. Enroll within 90 days for retroactive deposit.

F.2 FLIGHT CANCELLATION
Options: (a) Full refund within 7 business days (credit card) or 20 days (cash/check), including all ancillary fees. (b) Rebooking on next available Epsilon flight at no cost.
Partner Airline Rebooking: If next Epsilon flight is 6+ hours away, rebooking on partner airline at no cost.
Fare Class Protection: Apex rebooked in Apex (or Summit + downgrade compensation). Others: rebooked in same class or upgraded free if sold out.

F.3 HOTEL & MEAL VOUCHERS
Hotel (overnight delays): Explorer/Navigator/non-members: up to $175/night. Captain/Admiral: up to $250/night. Ground transportation included.
Meal vouchers: Breakfast (5-11AM): $15. Lunch (11AM-5PM): $20. Dinner (5PM-5AM): $25.

F.4 CONNECTING FLIGHT PROTECTION
Missed connection due to Epsilon delay (single PNR): Automatic rebooking, hotel/meal if overnight. Baggage auto-rerouted.
Customer-caused missed connections: Standard rebooking fees apply, no compensation.
Minimum Connection Times: Domestic-to-Domestic: 45 min. Domestic-to-International: 90 min. International-to-Domestic: 90 min. International-to-International: 120 min.

F.5 TARMAC DELAY RIGHTS
Deplaning offered after 3 hours (domestic) or 4 hours (international). Water and snacks after 2 hours. Updates every 30 minutes.

F.6 DENIED BOARDING — COMPENSATION FOR OVERSOLD FLIGHTS

F.6.1 Voluntary Denied Boarding
When a flight is oversold, Epsilon Airlines first solicits volunteers willing to give up their seats. Volunteers receive a travel credit of $200-$800 depending on delay length, rebooking on the next available flight, and a meal voucher.

F.6.2 Involuntary Denied Boarding Compensation
When passengers are involuntarily denied boarding on an oversold Epsilon Airlines flight, the following compensation applies based on arrival delay at the final destination:
- Arrival delay under 1 hour: no additional monetary compensation beyond rebooking.
- 1 to 2 hours domestic (1 to 4 hours international): 200% of one-way fare, minimum $400, maximum $800.
- 2 to 4 hours domestic (over 4 hours international): 400% of one-way fare, minimum $800, maximum $1,600.
- Over 4 hours domestic or no alternative offered: 400% of one-way fare plus 5,000 bonus SkyCredits.
All involuntarily denied boarding passengers also receive rebooking on the next available Epsilon Airlines flight and a meal voucher.

F.6.3 Denied Boarding Bumping Priority
Passengers are bumped in reverse loyalty order (last to be bumped first): Admiral > Captain > Navigator > Explorer > Non-member. Within a tier, passengers who checked in later are bumped first.

F.7 DOWNGRADE COMPENSATION
Apex to Summit: $200 travel credit + 3,000 SC. Summit to Horizon: $100 credit + 1,500 SC. Horizon to Essentials: $75 credit + 1,000 SC. Plus fare difference refund.

F.8 CREW-CAUSED DELAYS
Treated identically to mechanical delays for compensation purposes. Crew rest requirements are non-negotiable.

name: 07 — Special Services & Assistance
content:
SECTION G: SPECIAL SERVICES & ASSISTANCE

G.1 UNACCOMPANIED MINORS (UM)
Ages 5-7: UM service mandatory. Direct flights only. Fee: $150 each way. Not available under age 5.
Ages 8-11: UM service mandatory. Direct flights or single-connection. Fee: $150 each way.
Ages 12-14: UM service optional. Fee: $100 each way if elected.
Ages 15-17: No UM service required or available. Standard adult policies apply.

Flight Restrictions: Ages 5-7 not accepted on international flights. No UM on last flight of the day. No UM on red-eye flights (22:00-05:59).
Escort Procedures: Epsilon staff escorts UM from check-in through security, to gate, onto aircraft. At arrival, UM released only to designated pickup person with valid government ID listed on UM authorization form. If pickup person doesn't appear within 60 minutes, emergency contacts called. After additional 60 minutes, local authorities may be notified.
Capacity: Maximum 8 unaccompanied minors per flight.

G.2 PASSENGERS WITH DISABILITIES
G.2.1 Wheelchair assistance provided free on all flights. Request 48 hours in advance recommended.
G.2.2 Service Animals: Dogs trained for specific tasks, permitted in cabin free. Requires DOT Service Animal Transportation Form and vet health certificate within 1 year. Must be harnessed/leashed at all times.
G.2.3 Emotional Support Animals: NOT recognized as service animals. Must comply with standard pet policy (G.5) including all fees.
G.2.4 Accessible Seating: Bulkhead seats with movable armrests reserved for passengers with disabilities. Onboard aisle wheelchair on aircraft with 60+ seats.

G.3 MEDICAL CLEARANCE
Required when: Supplemental oxygen needed ($175/segment or bring own FAA-approved device), stretcher required ($3,500/segment + adjacent seats), communicable disease within 14 days, surgery within 10 days.
Form EPMC-1 completed by licensed physician, submitted to medical@epsilonairlines.com at least 72 hours before departure. Response within 24 hours.

G.4 PREGNANT TRAVELERS
Single pregnancy: No restrictions up to 36 weeks. 36-38 weeks: medical clearance letter required (dated within 72 hours of departure). After 38 weeks: no travel permitted.
Multiple pregnancy: No restrictions up to 32 weeks. 32-38 weeks: medical clearance required. After 38 weeks: no travel.

G.5 PET TRAVEL
In-Cabin: Small dogs and cats only. Carrier max 17x11x7.5 inches, pet+carrier max 20 lbs. Fee: $125 one-way. Max 2 pets per flight cabin. Not permitted in Apex Class cabin. Not on flights over 8 hours. Pet must remain in carrier entire flight.
Cargo: Dogs and cats, minimum 12 weeks old. IATA-compliant carrier. Fee: $250 domestic, $400 international. Temperature restrictions: not below 20°F or above 85°F. Brachycephalic (snub-nosed) breeds NOT accepted for cargo.
Service Animals: Exempt from all pet fees, carrier requirements, and cabin limits.

G.6 PREGNANT AND NURSING MOTHERS — BOARDING & SEATING PRIORITY
Priority boarding immediately after Zone 1, regardless of fare class. No documentation required.
Complimentary adjacent seat when available at check-in, free of charge.

name: 08 — Stratosphere Lounge & Onboard Services
content:
SECTION H: STRATOSPHERE LOUNGE & ONBOARD SERVICES

H.1 STRATOSPHERE LOUNGE ACCESS
Eligible: (a) Apex Class with same-day boarding pass. (b) Captain tier on same-day Epsilon flight. (c) Admiral tier at any time, regardless of airline or travel. (d) Epsilon Voyager Premium Card holders with same-day boarding pass.
Day Pass: $65 per person per visit. Non-refundable.
Hours: Generally 5:00 AM to 11:00 PM local time.

H.2 GUEST POLICY
Admiral tier: 2 guests or immediate family. Captain tier: 1 guest. Apex Class: 1 guest. Premium Card: 0 guests. Day pass: 0 guests.
Additional guests: $45 per person. Children under 2: free. Children 2-12: $25.

H.3 LOUNGE AMENITIES
Complimentary: Hot and cold buffet (rotating seasonal menu). Premium spirits and craft cocktails (full bar). Shower suites (30-minute slots, towels/toiletries provided). High-speed Wi-Fi (no login required). Printing/scanning (15 pages free). Phone rooms (private, soundproofed, 20-min courtesy limit).
Premium (additional charge): Spa treatments $35-$95. Private conference room $50/hour. A la carte dining $12-$30 per item.
Locations: Cascadia (CSC): 2 lounges (Terminal A, Terminal C). Meridian Bay (MRB): 1 lounge (Terminal 2). Solstice City (SLC): 1 lounge (International Terminal). 14 partner lounges worldwide.

H.4 ONBOARD WI-FI
Browse: $8.99/flight (messaging, basic web). Stream: $15.99/flight (video/music streaming). Full Flight Pass: $49.99/month (unlimited).
Complimentary: Captain/Admiral: Browse free. Admiral: Stream free.
SkyCredit redemption: Browse 3,000 SC. Stream 6,000 SC.
Available on all mainline aircraft. NOT available on Epsilon Express regional turboprops.

H.5 ONBOARD MEAL SERVICE
Essentials/Horizon (Domestic): Complimentary non-alcoholic beverages. Snack mix on flights 2+ hours. Buy-on-board menu $7-$14. Alcoholic beverages: $9 beer/wine, $11 spirits.
Summit (Domestic): Complimentary snack box, non-alcoholic beverages. Beer and wine $8.
Summit (International): Complimentary hot meal (2 entrees), beer and wine with meal, second meal on flights 8+ hours.
Apex (Domestic): Multi-course meal, premium open bar, warm towel service.
Apex (International): Pre-departure champagne, chef-curated 3-course dinner with wine pairing, mid-flight service on demand, turn-down service with premium bedding on 8+ hour flights, pre-arrival breakfast.

H.6 ALCOHOL POLICY
Minimum age 21. Economy cabin: max 2 alcoholic beverages per passenger per hour. Apex Class: no per-hour limit but responsible service applies. Outside alcohol strictly prohibited onboard.

H.7 ONBOARD CONDUCT POLICY
Level 1 Minor disruption: Verbal warning, documented.
Level 2 Sustained disruptive behavior: Travel ban 6-24 months.
Level 3 Interference with crew: Immediate flight diversion possible. Permanent travel ban. Law enforcement referral.
Smoking/vaping: $500 fine, 12-month travel ban minimum, FAA incident report.
Appeals: Written to conduct@epsilonairlines.com within 30 days. Safety Review Board decision within 45 business days.
