name: 10 — International Travel Regulations
content:
SECTION I: INTERNATIONAL TRAVEL REGULATIONS & REQUIREMENTS

I.1 TRAVEL DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENTS

I.1.1 Passport Requirements
All passengers traveling on international Epsilon Airlines flights must present a valid passport at the time of check-in. The passport must meet the following requirements:
- The passport must be valid for at least six (6) months beyond the date of intended return to the country of origin.
- The passport must contain at least two (2) blank visa pages for entry stamps.
- The passport must be in good condition with no significant damage, tears, or water stains that could render the biographical page unreadable.
- Machine-readable passports (MRP) are required for all Epsilon Airlines international routes. Handwritten or non-machine-readable passports are not accepted.

Epsilon Airlines will deny boarding to any passenger who does not present valid travel documentation. No refund or compensation is provided for denied boarding due to insufficient documentation; however, change fees may be waived at the discretion of the station manager.

I.1.2 Visa Requirements
Passengers are solely responsible for obtaining the appropriate visas for their destination country and any transit countries. Epsilon Airlines provides a visa information tool at www.epsilonairlines.com/visas powered by IATA Timatic, but this tool is advisory only and does not constitute a guarantee of entry.

Common visa scenarios on Epsilon Airlines routes:
- Cascadia to Windholm: No visa required for stays under 90 days for citizens of Cascadia, Meridian Bay, and Solstice City.
- Cascadia to Jade Harbor: Tourist visa required for all passengers. Must be obtained prior to travel. Electronic visa (e-visa) available through www.jadeharbor.gov/evisa, processing time 3-5 business days.
- Cascadia to Sakura City: Visa waiver for stays under 90 days for most Western passport holders. Registration required via Sakura Electronic Travel Authorization (SETA) system at least 72 hours before departure. Fee: $14 USD equivalent.
- Meridian Bay to Larkstone: No visa required for stays under 180 days.
- Meridian Bay to Opal Bay: Visa required. Must be obtained from Opal Bay consulate. Processing time: 10-15 business days. Expedited processing available for $80 additional fee.
- Solstice City to Briarvale: No visa required for stays under 90 days within any 180-day period.

Transit Visa Requirements: Passengers connecting through international hubs may require a transit visa even if they do not intend to leave the airport. Epsilon Airlines recommends checking transit visa requirements for all connection points on your itinerary.

I.1.3 APIS (Advance Passenger Information System)
Epsilon Airlines is required by law to collect and transmit Advance Passenger Information (API) data to the destination country's border authorities. The following data must be provided at the time of check-in or no later than 72 hours before departure via Manage Booking:
- Full legal name as it appears on the travel document
- Date of birth
- Gender
- Nationality
- Travel document type and number
- Travel document expiry date
- Country of issuance
- Country of residence
- Destination address (where applicable)

Failure to provide complete API data will result in denial of boarding. Epsilon Airlines recommends entering this information at the time of booking to avoid delays at check-in.

I.1.4 Customs Declarations
Passengers arriving on international Epsilon Airlines flights are responsible for completing all customs and immigration forms required by the destination country. On flights to certain destinations, Epsilon Airlines cabin crew will distribute customs declaration forms during the flight. Electronic customs declarations are available for select destinations through the Epsilon Airlines mobile app.

Duty-Free Allowances (general guidance — passengers must verify with destination country):
- Alcohol: Typically 1-2 liters per adult passenger
- Tobacco: Typically 200 cigarettes or 50 cigars per adult passenger
- Gifts/Merchandise: Varies by country, typically $400-$800 USD equivalent
- Currency: Declaration required for amounts exceeding $10,000 USD or equivalent in most countries

Prohibited items vary by destination. Epsilon Airlines strongly recommends reviewing destination country customs regulations before travel.

I.2 INTERNATIONAL BAGGAGE RULES

I.2.1 Free Checked Baggage — International Flights
International baggage allowances differ from domestic policies as follows:
- Essentials Fare International: One (1) complimentary checked bag up to 50 lbs (23 kg) is included on all international itineraries, regardless of the domestic policy.
- Horizon Fare International: One (1) complimentary checked bag up to 50 lbs (23 kg).
- Summit Fare International: Two (2) complimentary checked bags, each up to 50 lbs (23 kg).
- Apex Class International: Three (3) complimentary checked bags, each up to 70 lbs (32 kg).

I.2.2 Weight vs. Piece System
Epsilon Airlines uses the piece system for all routes. Some partner airlines in the CloudAlliance network use the weight system. When connecting from an Epsilon Airlines flight to a CloudAlliance partner flight:
- If the Epsilon Airlines flight is the longer segment, Epsilon baggage rules apply to the entire journey.
- If the partner airline flight is the longer segment, partner airline baggage rules apply.
- In cases of equal distance, the more generous allowance applies.

I.2.3 Restricted and Prohibited Items — International
In addition to standard restricted items, the following items are subject to additional international restrictions:
- Fresh fruits, vegetables, and meats: Subject to agricultural inspection and may be confiscated at destination.
- Dairy products: Restricted on routes to certain countries.
- Seeds, plants, and soil: Generally prohibited on all international routes.
- Medications: Passengers carrying prescription medications must have a doctor's letter and medications in original packaging. Controlled substances may require additional documentation from both origin and destination countries.
- Cultural artifacts and antiques: Export permits may be required from the origin country. Epsilon Airlines is not responsible for items confiscated by customs.

I.3 INTERNATIONAL FLIGHT OPERATIONS

I.3.1 Immigration Pre-Clearance
Epsilon Airlines offers immigration pre-clearance at the following airports, allowing passengers to clear destination country immigration and customs before departure:
- Cascadia International Airport (CSC): Pre-clearance available for flights to Larkstone and Briarvale.
- Meridian Bay International (MRB): Pre-clearance available for flights to Windholm.

Pre-clearance benefits: Passengers arrive at their destination as domestic arrivals, bypassing immigration queues. Pre-clearance is mandatory where available — passengers cannot opt out.

I.3.2 International Transfer and Connection Procedures
When connecting between international flights at an Epsilon Airlines hub:
- Minimum connection time for international-to-international connections: 120 minutes.
- Passengers must clear immigration and customs at the first point of entry, re-check bags (handled automatically for through-checked baggage on a single PNR), and proceed through security to the departing gate.
- Epsilon Airlines provides dedicated International Connection Assistants at Cascadia and Meridian Bay hubs to guide connecting passengers.
- Admiral and Captain tier members may use the dedicated Fast Track immigration lane where available.

I.3.3 Codeshare and Interline International Flights
Epsilon Airlines operates codeshare agreements with the following CloudAlliance partner airlines on international routes:
- Northern Wings (NW): Joint service on Cascadia-Windholm and Cascadia-Briarvale routes.
- Pacific Horizon Airways (PH): Joint service on Cascadia-Jade Harbor and Cascadia-Sakura City routes.
- Meridian Express (MX): Joint service on Meridian Bay-Larkstone and Meridian Bay-Opal Bay routes.

When traveling on a codeshare flight operated by a partner airline, the operating carrier's service standards, aircraft, and onboard products apply. Epsilon Airlines' baggage policies and Voyager Rewards earning rates still apply when booked under an Epsilon Airlines flight number (EA code).

I.3.4 International Flight Cancellation and Delay Rights
In addition to Epsilon Airlines' standard compensation policies (Section F), passengers on international routes may be entitled to additional rights under the laws of the destination or origin country:
- Flights to/from Windholm, Larkstone, or Briarvale: Passengers may be entitled to compensation of $300-$700 USD equivalent under the destination country's passenger rights regulations for cancellations and long delays, subject to extraordinary circumstances exemptions.
- Flights to/from Jade Harbor: Local regulations require airlines to provide meals and accommodation for delays exceeding 4 hours, regardless of cause.
- Flights to/from Sakura City: Compensation regulations similar to domestic policies. Additional meal vouchers for delays over 2 hours are mandated by local law.

Epsilon Airlines will apply whichever compensation standard — its own policies or local regulations — provides the greater benefit to the passenger.

I.4 CURRENCY AND PAYMENT — INTERNATIONAL TRANSACTIONS

I.4.1 Currency of Sale
All Epsilon Airlines fares are quoted in United States Dollars (USD). Passengers purchasing tickets from international websites may see fares displayed in local currency for convenience, but the charge will be processed in USD. Exchange rate fluctuations between the time of fare display and payment processing are the passenger's responsibility.

I.4.2 International Payment Methods
Accepted payment methods for international bookings:
- Major credit cards: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover
- Epsilon Airlines Travel Vouchers (issued in USD)
- SkyCredits (valued at standard redemption rates regardless of origin country)
- Wire transfer (for group bookings of 20 or more passengers only, must be received 14 business days before departure)

I.4.3 Refund Currency
Refunds for international tickets are processed in the original currency of payment. If the ticket was purchased in USD and the passenger's credit card is denominated in another currency, any exchange rate difference between the time of purchase and refund is borne by the passenger.

name: 11 — Aircraft Fleet & Route Network
content:
SECTION J: AIRCRAFT FLEET & ROUTE NETWORK

J.1 EPSILON AIRLINES FLEET OVERVIEW

Epsilon Airlines operates a modern fleet of narrow-body and wide-body aircraft across its domestic and international route network. The fleet is designed to provide consistent, comfortable service while maintaining fuel efficiency and environmental responsibility.

J.1.1 Narrow-Body Fleet

Boeing 737-800 (EA designation: E738)
Quantity in fleet: 45 aircraft
Seat configuration: 12 Summit (SkyStretch) + 150 Horizon/Essentials = 162 total
Range: 2,935 nautical miles (5,436 km)
Typical routes: Domestic short-haul and medium-haul
Amenities: Personal device entertainment (streaming to passenger devices), AC power at every seat in Summit section, USB-A and USB-C at every seat throughout, Gogo inflight Wi-Fi
Lavatory count: 3 (1 forward, 2 aft)
Overhead bin capacity: Roller bag capacity for approximately 120 passengers; remaining passengers may need to gate-check

Boeing 737 MAX 8 (EA designation: EM38)
Quantity in fleet: 30 aircraft
Seat configuration: 16 Summit (SkyStretch) + 153 Horizon/Essentials = 169 total
Range: 3,550 nautical miles (6,570 km)
Typical routes: Domestic medium-haul, Caribbean, Mexico, short transatlantic
Amenities: Boeing Sky Interior with LED mood lighting, larger windows, personal seatback entertainment screens in Summit section, USB-C at every seat, Gogo 5G Wi-Fi
Lavatory count: 3 (1 forward, 2 aft)
Fuel efficiency: 14% more fuel-efficient than 737-800 per seat-mile

Airbus A220-300 (EA designation: EA23)
Quantity in fleet: 20 aircraft
Seat configuration: 12 Summit (SkyStretch) + 128 Horizon/Essentials = 140 total
Range: 3,350 nautical miles (6,204 km)
Typical routes: Domestic short-haul, secondary market routes
Amenities: Widest single-aisle cabin (2-3 configuration in Horizon, 2-2 in Summit), extra-large overhead bins, panoramic windows, personal seatback entertainment screens at every seat, USB-C and AC power at every seat, Viasat Ka-band Wi-Fi
Lavatory count: 3 (1 forward, 2 aft)
Noise level: Significantly quieter than 737 family, averaging 10-15 dB lower in cabin

J.1.2 Wide-Body Fleet

Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner (EA designation: E789)
Quantity in fleet: 18 aircraft
Seat configuration: 30 Apex (CloudDeck Suite, 1-2-1) + 28 Summit (SkyStretch, 2-3-2) + 186 Horizon/Essentials (3-3-3) = 244 total
Range: 7,530 nautical miles (13,950 km)
Typical routes: Transatlantic, select transpacific
Amenities: CloudDeck Suite with fully lie-flat seat, suite door, 18-inch 4K entertainment screen. Summit SkyStretch with 36-inch pitch, 13.3-inch seatback screen. Economy with 32-inch pitch, 11.6-inch seatback screen. Electronically dimmable windows throughout. Lower cabin altitude (6,000 ft vs typical 8,000 ft) and higher humidity for reduced jet lag. Panasonic Aero Wi-Fi.
Lavatory count: 7 (2 Apex, 1 Summit, 4 Economy)
Special features: Onboard self-service beverage bar between Summit and Economy cabins. Premium amenity kits in Apex (Tumi branded) and Summit (Epsilon branded).

Airbus A330-900neo (EA designation: EA39)
Quantity in fleet: 12 aircraft
Seat configuration: 28 Apex (CloudDeck Suite, 1-2-1) + 32 Summit (SkyStretch, 2-3-2) + 198 Horizon/Essentials (3-3-3) = 258 total
Range: 6,550 nautical miles (12,130 km)
Typical routes: Transatlantic, South America, select transpacific
Amenities: CloudDeck Suite with Thompson Vantage XL lie-flat seat, privacy partition, 17.3-inch 4K entertainment screen. Summit SkyStretch with 35-inch pitch, 13.3-inch seatback screen. Economy with 31-inch pitch, 11.6-inch seatback screen. Airspace by Airbus cabin with LED ambient lighting. Panasonic eX3 entertainment system with Bluetooth audio. Viasat Ka-band Wi-Fi.
Lavatory count: 8 (2 Apex, 2 Summit, 4 Economy)

J.1.3 Regional Fleet (Epsilon Express)

Embraer E175 (EA designation: EE75)
Quantity in fleet: 35 aircraft (operated by Epsilon Express, a wholly-owned subsidiary)
Seat configuration: 12 Summit + 64 Horizon/Essentials = 76 total
Range: 2,000 nautical miles (3,704 km)
Typical routes: Short-haul domestic, hub connections from secondary airports
Amenities: 2-2 configuration throughout (no middle seats), Wi-Fi NOT available, complimentary snack service on all flights
Note: Epsilon Express flights are identified by flight numbers EA 5000-5999. Voyager Rewards earning rates are identical to mainline Epsilon Airlines flights. Stratosphere Lounge access is honored at all airports served by Epsilon Express.

J.2 HUB AIRPORTS

J.2.1 Cascadia International Airport (CSC) — Primary Hub
Terminal A: Domestic departures (Gates A1-A35)
Terminal B: Epsilon Express regional departures (Gates B1-B15)
Terminal C: International departures (Gates C1-C22)
Stratosphere Lounge locations: Terminal A (near Gate A12, 8,500 sq ft), Terminal C (near Gate C8, 6,200 sq ft)
Hub operations: 180+ daily departures, connecting bank structure with 5 connection complexes (waves) per day
Minimum connection times: Domestic-Domestic 45 min, Domestic-International 90 min, International-Domestic 90 min, International-International 120 min
Ground transportation: Epsilon Airlines shuttle bus connects Terminals A, B, and C every 8 minutes. Automated People Mover (APM) connects Terminal C to International Arrivals Hall.

J.2.2 Meridian Bay International Airport (MRB) — Secondary Hub
Terminal 2: All Epsilon Airlines domestic and international departures (Gates 2A-2H, Gates 2J-2P)
Stratosphere Lounge location: Terminal 2 (near Gate 2F, 5,800 sq ft)
Hub operations: 90+ daily departures
Focus cities served: Coral Ridge, Pinnacle Point, East Harbor, Larkstone, Opal Bay
Minimum connection times: Domestic-Domestic 40 min, Domestic-International 75 min

J.2.3 Solstice City Airport (SLC) — Focus City
International Terminal: Gates S1-S12
Stratosphere Lounge location: International Terminal (near Gate S6, 4,200 sq ft)
Operations: 45+ daily departures, primarily domestic with select international service to Briarvale
Note: Solstice City is a focus city, not a full hub. Limited connecting itineraries are available through Solstice City.

J.3 ROUTE NETWORK DETAILS

J.3.1 Domestic Routes
Epsilon Airlines serves 65 domestic destinations across the network. High-frequency routes (6+ daily flights):
- Cascadia (CSC) — Meridian Bay (MRB): 12 daily flights, mix of E738 and EM38
- Cascadia (CSC) — Solstice City (SLC): 8 daily flights
- Cascadia (CSC) — East Harbor (EHB): 8 daily flights, including 2 widebody (E789) services
- Meridian Bay (MRB) — Solstice City (SLC): 6 daily flights
- Meridian Bay (MRB) — Pinnacle Point (PPT): 6 daily flights
- Cascadia (CSC) — Coral Ridge (CRG): 6 daily flights

Medium-frequency routes (3-5 daily flights): 22 routes
Low-frequency routes (1-2 daily flights): 30 routes
Seasonal routes (summer only): 7 routes to leisure destinations

J.3.2 International Routes
Epsilon Airlines serves 18 international destinations. Service details:
- Cascadia — Windholm (WDH): Daily, E789, 8h 15m eastbound / 9h 45m westbound
- Cascadia — Larkstone (LKS): 5x weekly, A339, 9h 30m / 11h 00m
- Cascadia — Briarvale (BRV): Daily, E789, 7h 45m / 9h 15m
- Cascadia — Jade Harbor (JDH): Daily, E789, 11h 30m / 10h 45m
- Cascadia — Sakura City (SKC): Daily, A339, 10h 15m / 11h 30m
- Cascadia — Opal Bay (OPB): 4x weekly, E789, 14h 00m / 13h 15m
- Meridian Bay — Larkstone (LKS): Daily, A339, 8h 00m / 9h 30m
- Meridian Bay — Windholm (WDH): 5x weekly, E789, 7h 30m / 8h 45m
- Meridian Bay — Opal Bay (OPB): 3x weekly, A339, 15h 30m / 14h 45m
- Solstice City — Briarvale (BRV): 4x weekly, EM38 (seasonal A339 in summer), 8h 30m / 10h 00m

J.3.3 CloudAlliance Partner Network
Through Epsilon Airlines' membership in the CloudAlliance, passengers can connect to 450+ destinations worldwide via partner airlines:
- Northern Wings (NW): 120 destinations across Northern Europe and Scandinavia
- Pacific Horizon Airways (PH): 85 destinations across East Asia and Oceania
- Meridian Express (MX): 65 destinations across Southern Europe and North Africa
- Condor Air (CA): 95 destinations across Central and South America
- SilkRoute Airlines (SR): 90 destinations across South Asia and the Middle East

Codeshare flights: Epsilon Airlines places its EA code on select partner airline flights, allowing passengers to book these flights directly through epsilonairlines.com and earn Voyager Rewards SkyCredits.

J.4 FLEET SUSTAINABILITY PROGRAM

J.4.1 Environmental Commitments
Epsilon Airlines has committed to the following environmental targets:
- Net-zero carbon emissions by 2050
- 30% reduction in per-passenger-mile CO2 emissions by 2035 (vs. 2019 baseline)
- 10% sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) blend on all flights by 2030
- Single-use plastic elimination in cabin service by 2027

J.4.2 Carbon Offset Program
Passengers may purchase carbon offsets at the time of booking or through Manage Booking:
- Domestic short-haul: $3.50 per passenger
- Domestic long-haul: $7.00 per passenger
- Transatlantic: $18.00 per passenger
- Transpacific: $24.00 per passenger
SkyCredits may be used for carbon offsets at a rate of 500 SC per dollar.
Admiral tier members receive complimentary carbon offsets on all flights.

J.4.3 Fleet Renewal
Epsilon Airlines plans to retire all Boeing 737-800 aircraft by 2029, replacing them with Boeing 737 MAX 8 and Airbus A220-300 aircraft. This fleet transition will reduce average fuel consumption per seat-mile by 18% and noise footprint by 40%.

name: 12 — Digital Services & Technology
content:
SECTION K: DIGITAL SERVICES & TECHNOLOGY

K.1 EPSILON AIRLINES MOBILE APPLICATION

K.1.1 App Overview
The Epsilon Airlines mobile app is available for iOS (version 14.0 and later) and Android (version 10.0 and later). The app provides comprehensive travel management capabilities including booking, check-in, boarding passes, real-time flight status, Voyager Rewards account management, and in-flight entertainment control.

K.1.2 Mobile Booking Features
- Search and book flights across all fare classes (Essentials, Horizon, Summit, Apex)
- Flexible date search showing lowest fares across a 7-day window
- Price alerts: Set notifications for fare drops on specific routes
- Multi-city itinerary builder
- SkyCredits redemption booking
- Seat selection with interactive seat map showing real-time availability
- Ancillary purchase (bags, Wi-Fi, lounge passes) during booking or after
- Apple Pay, Google Pay, and saved credit card payment
- Booking confirmation and e-receipt delivered via push notification and email

K.1.3 Mobile Check-In
Mobile check-in opens 24 hours before scheduled departure and closes 45 minutes before departure (domestic) or 60 minutes before departure (international).

Check-in process:
1. Open the Epsilon Airlines app and navigate to "My Trips"
2. Select the applicable itinerary and tap "Check In"
3. Confirm passenger details and travel document information (international flights)
4. Select or change seat assignment (subject to availability and fare class rules)
5. Add checked baggage if desired (prepaid bag discount applies)
6. Review and accept dangerous goods declaration
7. Receive mobile boarding pass

Mobile boarding pass features:
- QR code updates automatically if gate changes occur
- Displays boarding zone, seat assignment, and departure time
- Supports Apple Wallet and Google Wallet for offline access
- TSA PreCheck / Known Traveler Number displayed when applicable
- Boarding pass sharing: Send boarding pass to travel companions via text or email

K.1.4 Real-Time Flight Tracking
- Push notifications for gate changes, delays, and cancellations
- Live aircraft position map showing current location, altitude, speed, and estimated arrival
- Inbound aircraft tracking: See where your aircraft is coming from and whether it's on time
- Weather overlay showing conditions at origin, destination, and along the route
- Baggage carousel assignment notification upon arrival

K.1.5 Voyager Rewards in the App
- View SkyCredits balance and transaction history
- Track progress toward next tier qualification
- View and manage UpLift Token inventory
- Request upgrades (cash, SkyCredits, or UpLift Token) directly from the app
- View upgrade waitlist position in real time
- Receive push notification when upgrade clears
- Access digital Voyager Rewards membership card
- Refer friends to Voyager Rewards and earn 2,000 bonus SkyCredits per successful referral

K.2 WEBSITE — EPSILONAIRLINES.COM

K.2.1 Online Booking Engine
The Epsilon Airlines website at www.epsilonairlines.com offers the full range of booking and travel management features. The website is optimized for modern browsers (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+).

Unique website features not available in the app:
- Multi-passenger booking with individual fare class selection (e.g., one passenger in Summit, companion in Horizon)
- Group booking request form for 10+ passengers
- EpsilonConnect corporate booking portal
- Travel agent booking interface (IATA-accredited agents only)
- Gift card purchase and redemption
- Detailed fare rules display for each booking class

K.2.2 Manage Booking Portal
Accessible via confirmation code (PNR) and last name, or through Voyager Rewards login. Capabilities include:
- View and modify itinerary (subject to fare rules and change fees)
- Add or change seat assignments
- Purchase checked baggage and other ancillaries
- Add Advance Passenger Information (API) for international travel
- Add Known Traveler Number or Redress Number
- Request special meals (must be requested at least 24 hours before departure)
- Request special assistance (wheelchair, etc.)
- Add or modify Voyager Rewards number
- Download or print itinerary receipt for expense reporting
- Cancel booking and process refund or travel credit

K.3 SELF-SERVICE KIOSKS

K.3.1 Airport Kiosk Network
Epsilon Airlines operates self-service kiosks at all airports it serves. Kiosks are located in the check-in area and are available for use beginning 24 hours before departure.

Kiosk capabilities:
- Check-in and boarding pass printing (paper boarding pass)
- Bag tag printing for checked luggage
- Seat assignment and changes
- Same-day flight changes (subject to fare rules and availability)
- Standby list registration
- Voyager Rewards account lookup and SkyCredits balance display
- Purchase checked baggage at prepaid rates

Kiosk limitations:
- Cannot process new bookings
- Cannot process refunds
- Cannot handle international document verification (must see agent)
- Cannot accommodate unaccompanied minors (must see agent)
- Cannot process pet travel (must see agent)

K.3.2 Bag Drop
After printing bag tags at the kiosk, passengers proceed to the Bag Drop counter. Bag Drop counters are staffed by Epsilon Airlines agents who verify bag weight, apply routing tags, and accept checked bags. Average bag drop processing time: 45 seconds per bag.

Automated Bag Drop (available at Cascadia and Meridian Bay hubs):
- Self-service bag drop machines accept tagged bags without agent assistance
- Facial recognition verification matches passenger to boarding pass
- Weight and size verification performed automatically
- Rejected bags (overweight, oversized, or security concerns) are directed to a staffed counter

K.4 IN-FLIGHT ENTERTAINMENT SYSTEM

K.4.1 Seatback Entertainment (Widebody and Select Narrowbody)
Available on: E789, A339, EM38 (Summit section only), EA23 (all seats)
Screen sizes: 18-inch 4K (Apex CloudDeck), 13.3-inch HD (Summit SkyStretch), 11.6-inch HD (Economy)
Content library: 200+ movies (updated monthly, including 30+ new releases), 400+ TV episodes, 80+ audio albums, 50+ podcasts, 20+ games, live TV channels (news and sports, where satellite coverage permits)
Bluetooth audio: Pair personal Bluetooth headphones/earbuds on E789 and A339 aircraft. Complimentary wired headphones available on all equipped aircraft.
Accessibility: Closed captioning on all content, audio description tracks on 50+ titles, screen reader compatibility, assistive listening device jack.

K.4.2 Streaming Entertainment (Personal Device)
Available on: E738 (all seats), EM38 (Economy section)
Passengers stream content to their personal devices (phone, tablet, laptop) via the Epsilon Airlines app or by navigating to entertainment.epsilonairlines.com in a web browser.
Content library: Same as seatback entertainment system, streamed via onboard Wi-Fi server. No internet connection required — content is stored on an onboard server.
Requirements: iOS 14+, Android 10+, or modern web browser. Passengers should download the Epsilon Airlines app before departure for the best experience.

K.4.3 Inflight Wi-Fi Connectivity
Epsilon Airlines offers Wi-Fi connectivity on all mainline aircraft. Wi-Fi is NOT available on Epsilon Express (EE75) regional aircraft.

Wi-Fi packages:
- Browse: $8.99 per flight segment. Includes messaging apps (iMessage, WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger), email, web browsing, and social media. Video streaming is throttled.
- Stream: $15.99 per flight segment. Includes everything in Browse plus video streaming (Netflix, YouTube, Disney+, etc.) at SD quality. Music streaming at full quality.
- Full Flight Pass: $49.99 per month (auto-renewing). Unlimited Browse + Stream on all Epsilon Airlines flights during the billing period. Can be purchased through the Epsilon Airlines app or website.

Complimentary Wi-Fi by tier:
- Explorer: No complimentary Wi-Fi
- Navigator: No complimentary Wi-Fi
- Captain: Complimentary Browse on all flights
- Admiral: Complimentary Stream on all flights

SkyCredits for Wi-Fi: Browse 3,000 SC, Stream 6,000 SC.

Wi-Fi speeds: Domestic flights use Gogo air-to-ground or Gogo 5G, providing speeds of 15-30 Mbps per aircraft. International flights use Panasonic Aero or Viasat Ka-band satellite, providing speeds of 12-50 Mbps per aircraft. Speeds are shared among all connected passengers and may vary based on demand and geographic location.

K.5 EPSILON AIRLINES CHATBOT — "SKYE"

K.5.1 Chatbot Capabilities
Epsilon Airlines' AI-powered chatbot "Skye" is available 24/7 through the mobile app, website, and Facebook Messenger. Skye can handle the following tasks without agent intervention:
- Flight status inquiries
- Booking lookup and itinerary display
- Check-in assistance
- Seat selection and changes
- Baggage policy questions
- Voyager Rewards balance and tier status queries
- SkyCredits earning rate calculations
- Basic flight change requests (within fare rules)
- Delay compensation eligibility check

K.5.2 Human Agent Handoff
For complex issues that Skye cannot resolve, the chatbot seamlessly transfers the conversation to a human agent. Transfer scenarios include:
- Refund processing
- Complex multi-segment itinerary changes
- Bereavement fare requests
- Medical clearance submissions
- Complaints and service recovery
- Group booking modifications

Human agent availability: 24/7 for Captain and Admiral tier. 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM Eastern Time for all other passengers. Average wait time for chat agent: 3 minutes (Captain/Admiral), 12 minutes (all others).

K.6 NOTIFICATIONS AND COMMUNICATION PREFERENCES

K.6.1 Notification Types
Epsilon Airlines sends the following types of notifications. Passengers can manage preferences through the app or website:
- Booking confirmation: Email (mandatory), push notification (optional)
- Check-in reminder: Push, email, or SMS (24 hours before departure)
- Gate change: Push notification (real-time), SMS (optional)
- Delay/cancellation: Push, SMS, and email (all active by default)
- Boarding: Push notification when boarding begins for your zone
- Upgrade cleared: Push notification and email
- SkyCredits earned: Push notification after each flight
- Tier qualification update: Email (monthly summary)
- Promotional offers: Email (opt-in, maximum 2 per week)

K.6.2 Do Not Disturb Hours
Passengers may set Do Not Disturb hours in the app, during which only critical operational notifications (cancellations, major delays, gate changes) will trigger push notifications or SMS. Promotional emails are never sent during DND hours.

name: 13 — Travel Insurance & Protection Plans
content:
SECTION L: TRAVEL INSURANCE & PROTECTION PLANS

L.1 EPSILON TRAVELGUARD PROTECTION PLANS

Epsilon Airlines offers three tiers of travel protection plans underwritten by Epsilon Insurance Partners, LLC. Plans may be purchased at the time of booking or up to 24 hours after booking. Plans cannot be added after the 24-hour post-booking window.

L.1.1 TravelGuard Basic
Price: $29 per passenger per round-trip (domestic), $49 per passenger per round-trip (international)
Coverage includes:
- Trip cancellation: Reimbursement of non-refundable ticket costs for covered reasons including illness, injury, death of immediate family member, jury duty, military deployment, natural disaster at destination, or terrorism event at destination. Maximum benefit: 100% of ticket cost.
- Trip interruption: Reimbursement of unused portion of trip plus one-way economy fare home. Maximum benefit: 150% of ticket cost.
- Travel delay: $100 per day for meals and accommodation when delay exceeds 6 hours due to covered reasons. Maximum benefit: $500.
- Baggage delay: $100 for essential purchases when baggage is delayed more than 12 hours. Maximum benefit: $200.
- Baggage loss/damage: Supplemental coverage above airline liability. Maximum benefit: $1,000.
- 24/7 emergency assistance hotline

L.1.2 TravelGuard Plus
Price: $59 per passenger per round-trip (domestic), $99 per passenger per round-trip (international)
All TravelGuard Basic coverage, plus:
- Trip cancellation for any reason (CFAR): Cancel for any reason up to 48 hours before departure and receive 75% reimbursement of non-refundable costs.
- Enhanced travel delay: $150 per day, maximum $750.
- Enhanced baggage delay: $200 for essential purchases when delay exceeds 6 hours. Maximum benefit: $500.
- Enhanced baggage loss/damage: Maximum benefit: $2,500.
- Missed connection: $500 for expenses when a covered delay causes a missed connection on a separate ticket.
- Emergency medical: $50,000 coverage for emergency medical treatment abroad.
- Emergency medical evacuation: $100,000 coverage for medical evacuation to nearest adequate facility.
- Rental car damage: $35,000 collision damage coverage for rental cars booked through Epsilon Airlines' car rental partners.

L.1.3 TravelGuard Premium
Price: $109 per passenger per round-trip (domestic), $189 per passenger per round-trip (international)
All TravelGuard Plus coverage, plus:
- Cancel for any reason (CFAR): 90% reimbursement (up from 75%).
- Pre-existing medical conditions: Coverage for trip cancellation due to pre-existing conditions, provided the plan is purchased within 14 days of initial trip deposit.
- Enhanced emergency medical: $250,000 coverage.
- Enhanced emergency evacuation: $500,000 coverage, including repatriation of remains.
- Travel delay: $200 per day, maximum $1,000.
- Baggage loss/damage: Maximum benefit: $5,000.
- Identity theft protection: 12-month identity monitoring service and $25,000 identity theft resolution coverage.
- Concierge service: 24/7 travel concierge for restaurant reservations, event tickets, and destination information.

L.2 FILING A TRAVELGUARD CLAIM

L.2.1 Claim Submission
Claims must be filed within 90 days of the incident. Claims may be submitted:
- Online: www.epsilonairlines.com/travelguard/claims
- By mail: Epsilon Insurance Partners, Claims Division, P.O. Box 44218, Cascadia, EA 98101
- By phone: 1-800-377-4570 (TravelGuard Claims Line), available Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM ET

Required documentation varies by claim type:
- Trip cancellation: Proof of reason (doctor's note, death certificate, jury summons, etc.), booking confirmation, proof of payment.
- Medical expenses: Itemized medical bills, proof of payment, attending physician's statement.
- Baggage claims: Airline property irregularity report (PIR), receipts for essential purchases, proof of item value for lost/damaged items.
- Travel delay: Airline confirmation of delay, receipts for meals and accommodation.

L.2.2 Claim Processing
Claims are reviewed within 10 business days of receipt of all required documentation. If additional information is needed, claimants are contacted via email. Approved claims are paid within 15 business days of approval via check or direct deposit (ACH).

L.2.3 Appeals
Denied claims may be appealed within 60 days of denial. Appeals must include additional documentation or a written explanation of why the denial should be reconsidered. Appeals are reviewed by a senior claims examiner within 20 business days.

L.3 EPSILON FLIGHTFLEX — FLEXIBLE BOOKING ADD-ON

L.3.1 FlightFlex Overview
FlightFlex is an alternative to travel insurance that provides enhanced change and cancellation flexibility. Unlike TravelGuard, FlightFlex does not provide medical, baggage, or delay coverage. FlightFlex is available for Essentials and Horizon fare classes only (Summit and Apex already include flexible change/cancellation policies).

L.3.2 FlightFlex Pricing
- Domestic flights: $19 per passenger per direction
- International flights: $29 per passenger per direction
- Must be purchased at the time of booking. Cannot be added later.

L.3.3 FlightFlex Benefits
- Unlimited free changes: Change date, time, or route with no change fee. Fare differences still apply. If new fare is lower, the difference is issued as a travel credit.
- Enhanced cancellation: Full travel credit for 100% of ticket value (no cancellation fee deducted), valid for 12 months.
- Same-day standby: Free same-day standby on any earlier or later flight on the same route, regardless of fare class or tier status.
- Same-day confirmed changes: $0 fee for same-day confirmed changes to any flight on the same route (subject to availability), regardless of tier status.

Note: FlightFlex does not convert a non-refundable ticket into a refundable ticket. Cancellations result in a travel credit, not a cash refund. For cash refund capability, passengers should purchase Summit or Apex fare or TravelGuard Plus/Premium protection.

L.4 BEREAVEMENT FARES

L.4.1 Eligibility
Epsilon Airlines offers discounted bereavement fares for travel due to the death or imminent death of an immediate family member. Immediate family is defined as: spouse, domestic partner, parent, child, sibling, grandparent, grandchild, parent-in-law, son/daughter-in-law, or legal guardian.

L.4.2 Discount
Bereavement fares provide a 20% discount off the lowest available Horizon Fare at the time of booking. The discount is applied to the base fare only (not taxes, fees, or ancillaries).

L.4.3 Booking
Bereavement fares must be booked by calling Epsilon Airlines reservations at 1-800-EPSILON (1-800-377-4566). They cannot be booked online or through the app. The following information is required:
- Name of deceased or critically ill family member
- Relationship to the passenger
- Name and address of hospital or funeral home
- Date of death or expected date of service/visitation

Documentation (death certificate, hospital letter, or funeral home confirmation) must be provided at check-in. If documentation cannot be presented, the fare difference between the bereavement fare and the standard fare will be charged.

L.4.4 Terms
- Bereavement fares are available for travel within 7 days of booking.
- Return travel must occur within 14 days of outbound travel.
- Bereavement fares earn SkyCredits at the Horizon Fare rate.
- Standard Horizon Fare change and cancellation policies apply.
- Change fees are waived for bereavement fare tickets.

L.5 TRAVEL CREDIT MANAGEMENT

L.5.1 Travel Credit Issuance
Travel credits may be issued in the following circumstances:
- Voluntary cancellation of a ticket (per fare class rules in Section D)
- Involuntary cancellation by Epsilon Airlines (full value, no fees)
- Delay compensation (per Section F)
- Customer service recovery (at agent discretion)
- Denied boarding (voluntary or involuntary, per Section F.6)
- FlightFlex cancellation (per Section L.3)

L.5.2 Travel Credit Rules
- Travel credits are issued in the name of the original passenger and are non-transferable to other passengers.
- Travel credits are valid for 12 months from the date of issue.
- Travel credits may be applied toward new bookings on any Epsilon Airlines-operated flight in any fare class.
- Multiple travel credits may be combined on a single booking, up to a maximum of 3 credits per transaction.
- If the new booking exceeds the credit value, the difference may be paid by any accepted payment method.
- If the new booking is less than the credit value, the remaining balance stays on the credit (no cash refund of unused credit balance).
- Travel credits cannot be used for ancillary purchases (baggage, Wi-Fi, lounge passes, etc.).
- Travel credits cannot be converted to SkyCredits.

L.5.3 Expired Travel Credit Reinstatement
Admiral tier members may reinstate an expired travel credit within 30 days of expiration by paying a $50 reinstatement fee. The reinstated credit is valid for an additional 6 months. Only one reinstatement is permitted per travel credit. Non-Admiral members cannot reinstate expired credits.

L.5.4 Travel Credit Lookup
Passengers can view all active travel credits by logging into their Voyager Rewards account on the website or app and navigating to "Payment Methods" > "Travel Credits." Travel credits not associated with a Voyager Rewards account can be looked up by calling 1-800-EPSILON with the credit reference number (printed on the issuance email or letter).

name: 14 — Safety, Security & Compliance
content:
SECTION M: SAFETY, SECURITY & COMPLIANCE

M.1 PASSENGER SAFETY BRIEFING

M.1.1 Pre-Flight Safety Demonstration
A safety demonstration is conducted before every Epsilon Airlines flight, either by cabin crew (live demonstration) or via safety video displayed on seatback screens or overhead monitors. Passengers are required by federal regulation to pay attention to the safety demonstration regardless of their familiarity with the aircraft type.

The safety demonstration covers:
- Seat belt operation (fastening, tightening, and releasing)
- Emergency exit locations and operation (overwing exits, forward and aft doors)
- Floor-level emergency lighting path
- Oxygen mask deployment and operation (in case of cabin depressurization)
- Life vest location and operation (overwater flights and flights within 50 miles of coastline)
- Brace position for emergency landing
- Electronic device stowage requirements during takeoff and landing
- Carry-on baggage stowage requirements
- Smoking and e-cigarette prohibition
- Lavatory smoke detector tampering prohibition (federal offense)

M.1.2 Safety Card
A laminated safety card specific to the aircraft type is located in the seatback pocket in front of each passenger. Passengers are encouraged to review the safety card before takeoff, paying attention to:
- Nearest emergency exit (count the number of seat rows to the nearest exit in case of low visibility)
- Exit operation specific to the aircraft type
- Brace position illustrations
- Emergency equipment locations

M.1.3 Crew Member Authority
The Captain (Pilot in Command) has final authority over the operation of the aircraft and the conduct of all persons on board, as mandated by federal aviation regulations. Cabin crew members are authorized to:
- Direct passengers to fasten seat belts
- Direct passengers to stow baggage and personal items
- Direct passengers to return to their seats
- Restrict movement in the cabin during turbulence or other safety events
- Confiscate prohibited items discovered during the flight
- Refuse service of alcoholic beverages
- Restrain passengers who pose a safety or security threat (using onboard restraint devices if necessary)

Failure to comply with crew member instructions is a violation of federal law and may result in arrest upon landing, civil penalties of up to $37,000 per violation, and/or criminal prosecution.

M.2 PROHIBITED ITEMS

M.2.1 Items Prohibited in Carry-On Baggage
The following items may NOT be carried in the aircraft cabin or in carry-on baggage:
- Firearms and ammunition (must be checked in accordance with M.2.3)
- Sharp objects with blades longer than 4 inches (knives, scissors with blades over 4 inches, box cutters, razor blades)
- Sporting equipment that could be used as a weapon (baseball bats, golf clubs, hockey sticks, ski poles)
- Tools longer than 7 inches (screwdrivers, wrenches, pliers, hammers)
- Self-defense items (pepper spray, mace, stun guns, martial arts weapons)
- Flammable liquids and aerosols (except personal care items in containers of 3.4 oz / 100 ml or less, in a single quart-size clear plastic bag)
- Explosive materials, fireworks, flares
- Compressed gases (except medically necessary oxygen approved under Section G.3)

M.2.2 Items Prohibited in Checked Baggage
The following items may NOT be placed in checked baggage:
- Lithium batteries (spare/loose): Must be carried in carry-on baggage. Batteries installed in devices may be checked.
- Electronic cigarettes and vaping devices: Must be carried in carry-on baggage (lithium battery fire risk).
- Flammable liquids exceeding 0.5 liters per container or 2.0 liters total
- Explosive materials of any kind
- Compressed gas cylinders (except small carbon dioxide cartridges for life vests, max 2 per passenger)
- Magnetic materials that could affect aircraft navigation systems
- Oxidizers and organic peroxides
- Radioactive materials (except limited quantities for medical devices)
- Infectious substances (Category A)

M.2.3 Firearms in Checked Baggage
Passengers may transport unloaded firearms in checked baggage in accordance with federal law and Epsilon Airlines policy:
- Firearm must be unloaded and locked in a hard-sided, manufacturer-approved case
- Ammunition must be in original packaging or a fiber, wood, or metal container specifically designed for ammunition
- Maximum 11 lbs (5 kg) of ammunition per passenger
- Passenger must declare the firearm at check-in by completing Epsilon Airlines Firearms Declaration Form (EA-FDF)
- Agent will inspect the firearm case (passenger opens the case for inspection)
- A "Declared Firearm" tag is placed inside the case (not visible externally)
- Fee: Standard checked bag fees apply. No additional firearm fee.
- Firearms are NOT permitted in carry-on baggage under any circumstances

M.3 SECURITY SCREENING

M.3.1 Transportation Security Administration (TSA) Screening
All passengers and carry-on baggage must pass through TSA security screening before entering the departure area. Epsilon Airlines recommends arriving at the airport:
- Domestic flights: At least 2 hours before departure
- International flights: At least 3 hours before departure
- During peak travel periods (holidays, summer): Add 30-60 minutes to the above recommendations

M.3.2 TSA PreCheck
Passengers enrolled in TSA PreCheck enjoy expedited screening benefits including:
- No need to remove shoes, belts, light jackets, or laptops
- No need to remove liquids bag
- Access to dedicated, shorter screening lines

Epsilon Airlines supports TSA PreCheck. Passengers must add their Known Traveler Number (KTN) to their Voyager Rewards profile or to each individual booking via Manage Booking. The KTN is then printed on the boarding pass, granting access to TSA PreCheck lanes.

Admiral and Captain tier members: Epsilon Airlines provides a $85 TSA PreCheck application fee credit once every 5 years. The credit is applied automatically to Voyager Rewards credit card statement or issued as a travel credit upon request.

M.3.3 Global Entry and Trusted Traveler Programs
Epsilon Airlines recommends Global Entry for frequent international travelers. Global Entry includes TSA PreCheck benefits. The Epsilon Voyager Premium Credit Card includes a Global Entry or TSA PreCheck application fee credit ($100) every 4 years.

M.4 HEALTH AND SAFETY PROTOCOLS

M.4.1 Aircraft Cleaning
All Epsilon Airlines aircraft undergo a thorough cleaning regimen:
- Turnaround clean (between flights): Seatback pockets emptied, tray tables and armrests wiped with disinfectant, lavatories cleaned and restocked, carpets vacuumed, visible debris removed. Duration: 15-25 minutes depending on aircraft size.
- Overnight deep clean: Full cabin cleaning including headrest covers, seat cushions, overhead bins, galleys, and lavatories. Carpet shampooing on a rotating schedule. Duration: 2-4 hours.
- Monthly detailed clean: Complete interior deep cleaning including walls, ceiling panels, window shades, and all hard surfaces. Air filtration system inspection. Duration: 6-8 hours.

M.4.2 Air Quality
All Epsilon Airlines mainline aircraft are equipped with HEPA (High Efficiency Particulate Air) filters that remove 99.97% of airborne particles including bacteria and viruses. Cabin air is completely refreshed every 2-3 minutes, with a mixture of fresh outside air (bleed air from engines) and recirculated filtered air.

M.4.3 Onboard Medical Equipment
All Epsilon Airlines aircraft carry the following medical equipment:
- Enhanced Emergency Medical Kit (EEMK): Includes medications (epinephrine, atropine, dextrose, diphenhydramine, nitroglycerin, aspirin, etc.), IV supplies, blood pressure cuff, stethoscope, CPR mask, and basic surgical supplies
- Automated External Defibrillator (AED): Located at the forward and aft crew stations on widebody aircraft, forward crew station on narrowbody
- First Aid Kit: Basic supplies for minor injuries (bandages, antiseptic, cold packs)
- Universal Precaution Kit: For handling bodily fluids (gloves, face masks, gowns, biohazard bags)

Cabin crew members are trained in first aid and AED operation. On international flights over 6 hours, Epsilon Airlines contracts with MedLink ground-based physician consultation service, allowing cabin crew to communicate with emergency physicians via satellite link.

M.5 DATA PRIVACY AND PASSENGER INFORMATION

M.5.1 Data Collection
Epsilon Airlines collects and processes the following categories of personal data:
- Booking data: Name, contact information, payment details, travel dates, fare class
- Voyager Rewards data: SkyCredits balance, tier status, travel history, preferences
- Operational data: Check-in time, seat assignment, boarding scan time, baggage tracking
- API data: Passport details, nationality, date of birth (for international travel, as required by law)
- Communication preferences: Email, SMS, push notification settings

M.5.2 Data Usage
Passenger data is used for:
- Providing and improving air transportation services
- Operating the Voyager Rewards loyalty program
- Complying with government regulations and law enforcement requests (API/APIS data, TSA Secure Flight, Customs and Border Protection)
- Sending operational communications (booking confirmations, gate changes, delays)
- Sending promotional communications (with passenger opt-in consent only)
- Aggregated analytics to improve route planning and service quality

M.5.3 Data Sharing
Epsilon Airlines shares passenger data with:
- Government authorities as required by law (TSA, CBP, foreign border agencies)
- CloudAlliance partner airlines when codeshare or interline travel is booked
- Service providers (ground handling, catering, hotel accommodation for IRROPS) on a need-to-know basis
- Payment processors for transaction processing
Epsilon Airlines does NOT sell passenger data to third parties for advertising purposes.

M.5.4 Data Retention
- Booking records: Retained for 3 years after travel completion
- Voyager Rewards data: Retained for the life of the account plus 2 years after account closure
- Financial transaction data: Retained for 7 years per accounting regulations
- API/APIS data: Retained for 5 years as required by government regulations

M.5.5 Passenger Rights
Passengers may request access to, correction of, or deletion of their personal data by contacting privacy@epsilonairlines.com or calling 1-800-EPSILON and selecting option 7 (Privacy). Requests are processed within 30 days. Certain data cannot be deleted when retention is required by law.

M.6 REGULATORY COMPLIANCE

M.6.1 Department of Transportation (DOT) Requirements
Epsilon Airlines complies with all DOT consumer protection regulations including:
- Full fare advertising (all taxes and fees included in displayed price)
- 24-hour booking hold or free cancellation
- Tarmac delay rules (3 hours domestic, 4 hours international)
- Denied boarding compensation
- Baggage liability limits
- Disability accommodation (ACAA compliance)
- Chronic delay reporting

M.6.2 Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) Requirements
Epsilon Airlines maintains an FAA Part 121 Air Carrier Certificate and complies with all applicable Federal Aviation Regulations (FARs) including:
- Aircraft maintenance and inspection programs
- Flight crew training and certification
- Cabin safety and emergency equipment requirements
- Flight time and duty time limitations for crew members
- Minimum Equipment List (MEL) and Configuration Deviation List (CDL) compliance

M.6.3 Filing a Complaint
Passengers who are unable to resolve a complaint through Epsilon Airlines customer service channels may file a complaint with:
- Department of Transportation Aviation Consumer Protection Division: https://www.transportation.gov/airconsumer
- Phone: 202-366-2220
- Mail: Aviation Consumer Protection Division, C-75, U.S. Department of Transportation, 1200 New Jersey Ave., SE, Washington, DC 20590

Epsilon Airlines takes all DOT complaints seriously and responds within 30 days as required by regulation.

name: 15 — Corporate & Group Travel Programs
content:
SECTION N: CORPORATE & GROUP TRAVEL PROGRAMS

N.1 EPSILONCONNECT CORPORATE TRAVEL PROGRAM

N.1.1 Program Overview
EpsilonConnect is Epsilon Airlines' dedicated corporate travel program, offering companies of all sizes volume-based discounts, enhanced booking tools, flexible travel policies, and dedicated support. Enrollment is free and available to any registered business entity.

N.1.2 Program Tiers

EpsilonConnect Standard
- Qualification: 50-199 flight segments per calendar year
- Fare discount: 3% off published Horizon and Summit fares
- Booking: Access to EpsilonConnect corporate booking portal at business.epsilonairlines.com
- Reporting: Quarterly travel spend and activity reports
- Support: Dedicated corporate support email (corporate@epsilonairlines.com), response within 4 business hours
- Flexibility: Company-wide waiver of Horizon Fare change fees (fare differences still apply)
- Voyager Rewards: Employees earn full SkyCredits on corporate bookings

EpsilonConnect Plus
- Qualification: 200-499 flight segments per calendar year
- Fare discount: 5% off published Horizon and Summit fares, 3% off Apex Class
- Booking: Corporate booking portal with policy compliance engine (set fare class limits, advance purchase requirements, and preferred routes)
- Reporting: Monthly detailed reports with carbon emissions tracking, cost center allocation, and traveler compliance metrics
- Support: Named account manager, dedicated phone line (1-800-377-4572), response within 2 business hours
- Flexibility: Company-wide waiver of all change fees on all fare classes. Unused ticket value fully transferable to any employee within the company.
- Lounge: 20 Stratosphere Lounge day passes per year, distributed at the Travel Manager's discretion
- Voyager Rewards: Employees earn full SkyCredits. Company earns 2 SkyCredits per $1 of corporate travel spend into a company SkyCredits pool.

EpsilonConnect Premier
- Qualification: 500+ flight segments per calendar year
- Fare discount: 8-15% off published fares across all fare classes (negotiated rate, reviewed annually)
- Booking: Full-featured corporate booking portal with multi-traveler booking, automated policy enforcement, PNR sharing, and integration with major travel management companies (TMCs) including Concur, Egencia, and Navan
- Reporting: Real-time dashboard with advanced analytics, YoY comparison, route optimization recommendations, and carbon offset tracking. Custom reports available on request.
- Support: Senior named account manager, priority phone line with <30-second average wait time, 24/7 emergency support
- Flexibility: Fully flexible change and cancellation on all fare classes with no fees. Unused ticket value transferable to any employee. Travel credits never expire while the EpsilonConnect Premier agreement is active.
- Lounge: Unlimited Stratosphere Lounge day passes for all employees traveling on Epsilon Airlines on the day of travel. 50 standalone Lounge passes per year for non-travel-day use.
- Voyager Rewards: Employees earn full SkyCredits at an enhanced rate of 1.25x base. Company earns 3 SkyCredits per $1 into company pool. Company pool SkyCredits can be redeemed for employee reward flights, upgrades, or gift cards.
- Upgrades: 12 complimentary one-cabin upgrade certificates per year (Horizon to Summit or Summit to Apex), valid on any employee's booking.
- Meetings & Events: Dedicated meetings and events coordinator for corporate retreats, conferences, and incentive travel. Group rates with additional 5% discount for events of 50+ passengers.

N.1.3 Corporate Booking Portal Features
The EpsilonConnect corporate booking portal (business.epsilonairlines.com) provides:
- Company travel policy integration: Set rules for maximum fare class, advance purchase minimums, preferred routes, and budget limits. Out-of-policy bookings require manager approval workflow.
- Cost center and project code allocation: Assign travel costs to departments, projects, or clients at the time of booking.
- Multi-passenger booking: Book flights for multiple employees simultaneously with individual seat and ancillary preferences.
- Traveler profiles: Store employee passport details, Voyager Rewards numbers, seat preferences, meal preferences, and Known Traveler Numbers.
- Unused ticket tracking: Dashboard showing all unused tickets and travel credits company-wide, with expiration date alerts.
- Invoice management: Consolidated monthly invoicing (net-30 terms for EpsilonConnect Plus and Premier). Individual trip receipts downloadable for expense reporting.
- API integration: REST API available for EpsilonConnect Premier customers to integrate booking data with internal travel and expense management systems.

N.2 GROUP TRAVEL

N.2.1 Group Booking Eligibility
Group bookings are available for parties of 10 or more passengers traveling on the same flight(s). Group bookings are available on Horizon, Summit, and Apex Class fares only (Essentials Fare is not available for group bookings).

N.2.2 Group Fare Discounts
- 10-19 passengers: 5% discount off published fares
- 20-49 passengers: 8% discount off published fares
- 50-99 passengers: 10% discount off published fares
- 100+ passengers: Custom negotiated rate (contact groups@epsilonairlines.com or 1-800-377-4575)

Discounts apply to the base fare only (not taxes, fees, or ancillaries). Group fares are not combinable with EpsilonConnect corporate discounts; the higher discount applies.

N.2.3 Group Booking Process
1. Submit group travel request via www.epsilonairlines.com/groups or by calling 1-800-377-4575
2. Provide: requested flight(s), number of passengers, fare class preference, and contact information
3. Epsilon Airlines Group Travel team provides a quote within 2 business days
4. Quote is valid for 7 days from issuance
5. To confirm: Submit signed group agreement and 10% non-refundable deposit
6. Passenger names must be provided:
   - 50% of names: At least 45 days before departure
   - 100% of names: At least 14 days before departure
   - Name changes: Permitted up to 72 hours before departure at no charge
7. Final payment due 30 days before departure

N.2.4 Group Baggage
Group bookings follow standard fare class baggage policies. Groups of 20+ may negotiate additional complimentary checked bags as part of the group agreement.

N.2.5 Group Seating
Epsilon Airlines makes best efforts to seat group members together. Groups of 10+ are guaranteed contiguous seating blocks when booked at least 60 days before departure. Seat assignments are provided 30 days before departure.

N.2.6 Charter Flights
For groups of 100+ passengers or for travel to destinations not served by scheduled Epsilon Airlines flights, charter flights may be arranged. Charter inquiries should be directed to charters@epsilonairlines.com. Typical charter pricing:
- Embraer E175 (76 seats): From $18,000 per flight hour
- Boeing 737 MAX 8 (169 seats): From $32,000 per flight hour
- Boeing 787-9 (244 seats): From $65,000 per flight hour
Charter flights earn SkyCredits at the Horizon Fare rate.

N.3 MEETINGS AND EVENTS

N.3.1 Event Travel Coordination
Epsilon Airlines' Meetings & Events team provides end-to-end travel coordination for corporate events, conferences, incentive trips, and group travel. Services include:
- Dedicated booking page with company branding and event information
- Negotiated group fares for event attendees
- Real-time attendee booking tracking dashboard
- Arrival and departure manifests for ground transportation planning
- Customized welcome announcements on flights with high event attendee counts
- Branded amenity kits or welcome gifts (for groups of 50+ in Summit or Apex, additional cost applies)

N.3.2 Event Fare Programs
For qualifying events (75+ attendees expected to fly Epsilon Airlines), the Events team creates a custom fare program:
- Published fare minus negotiated discount (typically 8-15%)
- Extended 72-hour hold on event fares (vs. standard 24-hour hold)
- Reduced change fees: $0 change fee for all attendees (fare differences still apply)
- Group block: A set number of seats are held at the discounted fare until a specified release date (typically 30 days before the event)

Contact: events@epsilonairlines.com or 1-800-377-4576

N.4 TRAVEL MANAGEMENT COMPANY (TMC) INTEGRATION

N.4.1 Supported TMC Platforms
Epsilon Airlines content is available through all major Global Distribution Systems (GDS):
- Amadeus: Full content including Epsilon Airlines private fares and ancillaries
- Sabre: Full content
- Travelport (Galileo/Worldspan/Apollo): Full content

N.4.2 NDC (New Distribution Capability)
Epsilon Airlines supports IATA NDC standard for direct distribution of fares and ancillaries. NDC-connected TMCs and online travel agencies receive:
- Access to Epsilon Airlines' lowest fares (price parity with epsilonairlines.com)
- Rich content including seat maps, fare breakdowns, and ancillary pricing
- Real-time booking confirmation and ticket issuance
- Post-booking servicing (changes, cancellations, ancillary additions)

NDC-certified partners receive a $2 per segment booking incentive, reducing net ticket cost for corporate clients.

N.4.3 Duty of Care Integration
Epsilon Airlines provides passenger location data to authorized TMCs and corporate security providers for duty of care purposes. When activated by the corporate client:
- Real-time traveler location (city-level, based on booking data)
- Flight status alerts sent to corporate travel manager or security team
- Emergency communication capability: Send messages to all company travelers in a specific region
- Disruption alerts: Automatic notification when employees are affected by delays, cancellations, or irregular operations

This service is available to EpsilonConnect Plus and Premier customers and requires a signed data sharing agreement. Contact your EpsilonConnect account manager to activate.
